name: Optomix rev2 2016
description: Two-channel vactrol low-pass gate with STRIKE and DAMP. rev2 adds audio-rate DAMP (sidechain compression), CV offsets on signal inputs, and drops CH1→CH2 normalization. Chainable via AUX.

controls.name: DAMP
controls.description: Sets ringing/decay response. CCW: long ringing. CW: muted, fast. Doubles as attenuator when DAMP CV In is patched.

controls.name: CONTROL
controls.description: Level knob for the vactrol VCA. Becomes attenuator for CONTROL Signal In when patched. Normalizes to +8V offset at jack.

inputs.name: CH 1 / CH 2 Signal In
inputs.description: Direct-coupled audio or CV input. rev2: no longer normalled CH 1 OUT → CH 2 IN.

inputs.name: CH 1 / CH 2 DAMP CV
inputs.description: CV input for DAMP parameter. Audio-rate capable (rev2) for sidechain compression. DAMP knob acts as attenuator when patched.

inputs.name: CH 1 / CH 2 CONTROL
inputs.description: Direct-coupled, highly-sensitive CV for the vactrol VCA. CONTROL knob acts as attenuator when patched.

inputs.name: CH 1 / CH 2 STRIKE
inputs.description: Gate input that plucks the vactrol — slow natural decay under DAMP control. No envelope generator needed.

inputs.name: AUX
inputs.description: Direct-coupled summing input. Chain multiple Optomix/modDemix into SUM.

outputs.name: CH 1 / CH 2 Signal Out
outputs.description: LPG-processed channel output. Amplitude and frequency content both respond to CONTROL.

outputs.name: SUM Out
outputs.description: Mix of CH 1 + CH 2 + AUX. Master output for chained setups.

behaviors.name: LPG (amplitude + frequency linked)
behaviors.description: Simultaneous VCA + non-resonant LPF. As CONTROL goes positive, amplitude rises with low frequencies opening first; as it falls, highs disappear before lows. Creates natural acoustic-like attack/decay like a struck drum or xylophone bar.

behaviors.name: STRIKE with DAMP decay
behaviors.description: 8V gate plucks the vactrol. Decay shape set by DAMP: CCW for long ringing, CW for fast muted pluck. Emulates drum-head palm-muting or guitar-string palm-mute without an envelope generator — the LPG becomes its own AD contour.

behaviors.name: Sidechain compression (rev2)
behaviors.description: Audio-rate at DAMP causes gain reduction. CONTROL sets the compression ratio (best ~40–60%). DAMP knob sets threshold. Soft, squishy ducking — route a kick's channel out into another channel's DAMP to duck a drone.

behaviors.name: DAMP as accent
behaviors.trigger: accent gate → STRIKE + accent mult → DAMP CV
behaviors.description: In sequencer patches, STRIKE alone accents by opening the LPG harder. Multing the accent gate to DAMP CV adds brightness/speed to accented steps, simulating velocity-sensitive attack. Non-accented env goes to CONTROL for body.

behaviors.name: Envelope generator (rev2)
behaviors.description: Because CH signal inputs carry a +8V offset when unpatched, applying a gate to STRIKE and taking the channel OUT yields an envelope. Fall time = DAMP. Rise is fixed-fast. Works in a pinch as an AD envelope with VC decay.

behaviors.name: CV offset / slew limiter
behaviors.trigger: signal → Signal In, CONTROL Combo Pot as level
behaviors.description: +8V offset normalization on Signal In allows use as an offset generator, attenuator, or summer. An Optomix channel can also be a simple slew limiter with DAMP setting fall time (rise stays fast).

behaviors.name: Vactrol speed characteristics
behaviors.description: Vactrols have moderate attack, slow decay, and non-matched gain between channels. Not suitable for clicks/pops or matched-gain stereo VCA duty. The 'ringing' sounds — especially on FM or ring-modulated sources — are the core character.

behaviors.name: AUX chaining
behaviors.trigger: SUM Out of another LPG → AUX
behaviors.description: Chain Optomix → Optomix or Optomix ↔ modDemix via AUX for larger mixes without external summing. AUX feeds SUM directly and is direct-coupled (CV works too).

patchIdeas.name: Bongo (PP + FM + LPG)
patchIdeas.patch: Touchplate-played bongo: FM source into CH 1, Pressure Points Common Gate strikes, CH 3 Gate adds dampening mid-decay.
steps: On your VCO, patch a sine wave into its own Linear FM input to make a two-operator FM tone.
Patch the VCO main out → Optomix CH 1 Signal In.
Set CH 1 CONTROL knob full CCW (so the vactrol is closed until struck).
Set CH 1 DAMP knob to ~9 o'clock (gentle dampening baseline).
Patch Pressure Points Common Gate → Optomix CH 1 STRIKE.
Patch Pressure Points CH 3 Gate → Optomix CH 1 DAMP CV.
Patch Optomix CH 1 Signal Out → your monitor input.
Tap PP touchplate 4 to pluck the bongo; tap touchplate 3 during decay to palm-mute.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Optomix CH 1 Signal Out — plucked FM bongo tone, up to 10Vpp.
patchIdeas.hear: Each touchplate 4 tap produces a short, woody FM bongo with natural decay. Holding touchplate 3 during the tail clamps down on the ringing like a hand on a drum head. The DAMP knob sets how hard touchplate 3 mutes — CW is drier, CCW is barely there.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Optomix:
* CH 1 CONTROL: full CCW
* CH 1 DAMP: ~30% (set dampening)

- VCO (Out) -> Optomix.CH 1 (Signal In) // FM source
- Pressure Points (Common Gate) g> Optomix.CH 1 (Strike) // touchplate 4
- Pressure Points (CH 3 Gate) g> Optomix.CH 1 (DAMP CV) // touchplate 3 dampens
- Optomix.CH 1 (Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: FM pings
patchIdeas.patch: CH 1 processes a sine, its output drives another VCO's Linear FM input. Each STRIKE trigger pings a short FM burst.
steps: Patch VCO A sine out → Optomix CH 1 Signal In (audio-rate).
Set CH 1 CONTROL knob full CCW — the vactrol stays closed at rest.
Set CH 1 DAMP knob to taste (12 o'clock is a good start).
Patch a trigger/gate source → Optomix CH 1 STRIKE.
Patch Optomix CH 1 Signal Out → VCO B Linear FM input.
Play a note on VCO B and fire triggers — each trigger pings a burst of FM.
Turn DAMP CW for tight, percussive clicks; turn CCW for longer modulated sweeps.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Optomix CH 1 Signal Out — pinged sine, gated by the vactrol, into VCO B Linear FM.
patchIdeas.hear: Every trigger drops a quick, decaying modulation burst into VCO B's pitch/timbre. With DAMP low you get a percussive 'chirp'; with DAMP high you get a longer FM sweep per strike. Works with LFO, random, or noise sources as the CH 1 input for chaotic bursts.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Optomix:
* CH 1 CONTROL: full CCW
* CH 1 DAMP: to taste

- VCO A (Sine) -> Optomix.CH 1 (Signal In)
- Trigger Source (Trig) g> Optomix.CH 1 (Strike)
- Optomix.CH 1 (Out) >> VCO B (Lin FM) // dynamic FM ping

patchIdeas.name: Kick-drum ducked drone
patchIdeas.patch: CH 1 generates a kick whose output feeds CH 2's DAMP CV. Drone on CH 2 is sidechain-ducked; monitor SUM.
steps: Patch a sine VCO → Optomix CH 1 Signal In (kick source).
Patch your kick gate → Optomix CH 1 STRIKE AND → the same VCO's Linear FM input (for pitch-drop thump).
Set CH 1 CONTROL knob full CCW and CH 1 DAMP to ~10 o'clock (short kick decay).
Patch a drone source → Optomix CH 2 Signal In.
Set CH 2 CONTROL knob to ~50% (this sets the compression ratio).
Set CH 2 DAMP knob starting at ~12 o'clock (this sets the threshold).
Patch Optomix CH 1 Signal Out → Optomix CH 2 DAMP CV (audio-rate sidechain).
Patch Optomix SUM Out → your monitor; adjust CH 2 DAMP and CONTROL to taste.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Optomix SUM Out — kick plus ducked drone, up to 10Vpp.
patchIdeas.hear: The kick hits, and the drone ducks underneath it with a soft, squishy pump — then swells back in as the kick tail fades. CH 2 CONTROL around 40–60% gives the best ratio; DAMP sets how loud the kick needs to be before ducking kicks in.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Optomix:
* CH 1 CONTROL: full CCW
* CH 2 CONTROL: ~50%
* CH 2 DAMP: sets threshold

- VCO (Sine) -> Optomix.CH 1 (Signal In)
- Gate (Kick) g> Optomix.CH 1 (Strike)
- Gate (Kick) g> VCO (Lin FM)
- Drone (Out) -> Optomix.CH 2 (Signal In)
- Optomix.CH 1 (Out) -> Optomix.CH 2 (DAMP CV) // sidechain
- Optomix (SUM Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Accent via dual envelope
patchIdeas.patch: Non-accent envelope → CONTROL for baseline dynamics; accent gate → STRIKE and → DAMP CV for louder, brighter accented steps.
steps: Patch your VCO → Optomix CH 1 Signal In.
Patch the sequencer step gate → your AD envelope's trigger.
Patch the AD envelope out → Optomix CH 1 CONTROL.
Set CH 1 CONTROL knob to ~70% (this attenuates the baseline envelope — leaves headroom for accents).
Patch the accent gate → Optomix CH 1 STRIKE.
Mult the accent gate → Optomix CH 1 DAMP CV.
Set CH 1 DAMP knob to at least 20% (adds snap/brightness on accented steps).
Patch Optomix CH 1 Signal Out → your monitor.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Optomix CH 1 Signal Out — sequenced voice with velocity-style accents.
patchIdeas.hear: Non-accented steps play at a normal level via the AD envelope. Accented steps jump in volume (STRIKE pings the vactrol harder) AND get a tighter, brighter attack (accent gate on DAMP speeds the decay). Classic velocity feel without a dedicated VCA.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Optomix:
* CH 1 CONTROL: ~70%
* CH 1 DAMP: ~20%

- Sequencer (Gate) g> Envelope (Trig)
- Envelope (Out) >> Optomix.CH 1 (CONTROL)
- Accent (Gate) g> Optomix.CH 1 (Strike)
- Accent (Gate) g> Optomix.CH 1 (DAMP CV)
- VCO (Out) -> Optomix.CH 1 (Signal In)
- Optomix.CH 1 (Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Palm-mute percussion
patchIdeas.patch: Gate strikes CH 1 AND triggers MATHS; MATHS' slow rise into DAMP CV clamps the ringing a moment after the pluck.
steps: Patch your VCO → Optomix CH 1 Signal In.
Patch your gate source → Optomix CH 1 STRIKE.
Mult the same gate → MATHS CH 1 (or CH 4) TRIGGER input.
Set MATHS CH 1 RISE to at least 60% (long rise — delays the mute).
Set MATHS CH 1 FALL to less than 50% (quick return).
Patch MATHS CH 1 Unity Out → Optomix CH 1 DAMP CV.
Set Optomix CH 1 DAMP knob to at least 65%.
Patch Optomix CH 1 Signal Out → your monitor.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Optomix CH 1 Signal Out — plucked tone with delayed palm-mute clamp.
patchIdeas.hear: Each gate plucks the vactrol and opens the tone brightly; a moment later MATHS' rising envelope arrives at DAMP and clamps the ringing down — exactly like a palm landing on a guitar string after the pluck. Raise MATHS Rise for later mutes, raise DAMP knob for a harder mute.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Optomix:
* CH 1 DAMP: ~65%

MATHS:
* CH 1: Rise ≥60%, Fall <50%

- Gate Source (Gate) g> Optomix.CH 1 (Strike)
- Gate Source (Gate) g> MATHS.CH 1 (Trig)
- MATHS.CH 1 (OUT) >> Optomix.CH 1 (DAMP CV) // delayed palm mute
- VCO (Out) -> Optomix.CH 1 (Signal In)
- Optomix.CH 1 (Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Two-voice LPG mixer
patchIdeas.patch: Voice 1 → CH 1, Voice 2 → CH 2, each with own STRIKE and DAMP. SUM Out gives the mix; AUX can chain another LPG.
steps: Patch Voice 1 → Optomix CH 1 Signal In.
Patch Voice 1's gate → Optomix CH 1 STRIKE.
Set CH 1 CONTROL full CCW and CH 1 DAMP tight (CW) for a short pluck.
Patch Voice 2 → Optomix CH 2 Signal In.
Patch Voice 2's gate → Optomix CH 2 STRIKE.
Set CH 2 CONTROL full CCW and CH 2 DAMP CCW for a long, ringing tail.
(Optional) Patch another Optomix/modDemix SUM Out → Optomix AUX to chain it in.
Patch Optomix SUM Out → your monitor.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Optomix SUM Out — mixed CH 1 + CH 2 + AUX, up to 10Vpp.
patchIdeas.hear: Two independently plucked voices share one output — one tight and staccato, the other long and bell-like — with no external mixer required. Chaining via AUX lets several LPG voices fan into one master SUM.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Optomix:
* CH 1 DAMP: tight
* CH 2 DAMP: long ringing

- Voice 1 (Out) -> Optomix.CH 1 (Signal In)
- Gate 1 (Trig) g> Optomix.CH 1 (Strike)
- Voice 2 (Out) -> Optomix.CH 2 (Signal In)
- Gate 2 (Trig) g> Optomix.CH 2 (Strike)
- Optomix (SUM Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: LPG as AD envelope source
patchIdeas.patch: Leave CH 2 Signal In unpatched (+8V offset). Strike CH 2 and the channel output becomes an AD envelope with VC decay.
steps: Leave Optomix CH 2 Signal In unpatched (the +8V normalled offset is the envelope's ceiling).
Set CH 2 CONTROL knob full CW (passes the full +8V offset through).
Set CH 2 DAMP knob to your desired decay time (CCW long, CW short).
Patch your gate source → Optomix CH 2 STRIKE.
Patch Optomix CH 2 Signal Out → your target CV input (VCA CV, filter cutoff, etc).
(Optional) Patch a CV → CH 2 DAMP CV for voltage-controlled decay time.
Fire gates — each one produces one AD envelope shot at the output.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Optomix CH 2 Signal Out — AD envelope, ~0–8V, fixed-fast rise + DAMP-controlled fall.
patchIdeas.hear: A clean AD envelope shaped like any vactrol LPG decay — fast attack, natural exponential-ish fall. Great as a spare envelope when no EG is free. Modulating DAMP CV gives voltage-controlled decay time.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Optomix:
* CH 2 CONTROL: full CW
* CH 2 DAMP: decay time
* CH 2 Signal In: unpatched (+8V offset)

- Gate Source (Gate) g> Optomix.CH 2 (Strike)
- Optomix.CH 2 (Out) >> VCA (CV) // Optomix as EG
- VCO (Out) -> VCA (In)
